nfrs.statement: Existing stock data survives every schema migration with no loss; migrations are additive, and each stock record carries an unmodifiable created_at timestamp and actor.
nfrs.fitness_function: Real-branch integration test: apply Alembic migration to the paired branch over seeded rows, assert pre-existing records and their immutable timestamp/actor are intact.

nfrs.statement: Stock quantity never persists below zero. (Overcommit/allocation is out of S1 scope; the non-negative invariant is the part F1 record/write must uphold.)
nfrs.fitness_function: Real-branch integration test: a write with negative resulting quantity is rejected at write time; no negative row is ever stored.

nfrs.statement: Every (sku, location) pair is uniquely addressable; a collision is resolved at write time by updating the existing record (upsert), never two stored duplicates and never an error page.
nfrs.fitness_function: Real-branch integration test: a DB unique constraint on (sku, location) plus a service-level upsert; submitting the same pair twice yields one row updated (covers AC4).

nfrs.statement: Integration tests run against the paired Lakebase branch, never a mock or in-memory substitute; CI refuses to merge a PR whose integration tests do not run against a real branch.
nfrs.fitness_function: pytest-bdd suite binds to the branch DB via conftest fixtures; CI gate asserts the real-branch connection (DB_NAME=stockflow) is used.

nfrs.statement: DB connection resolves from env (DB_NAME / PGDATABASE = stockflow, NOT databricks_postgres) so local preview follows the checked-out paired branch (twelve-factor config-in-env).
nfrs.fitness_function: Config-in-env fitness test: no hardcoded DSN; app reads DB_NAME/PGDATABASE and connects to stockflow.

nfrs.statement: Validation messages name the offending field (preference); a record missing optional detail renders 'not tracked' rather than null-crashing (preference).
nfrs.fitness_function: Integration/UI test: invalid submit returns a field-named inline error; a row with null optional fields renders an explicit 'not tracked'.

nfrs.statement: The SKU detail view reflects every committed stock record for that SKU across all its locations (no stale or omitted location) and reads only through the repository, never bypassing the ORM layer.
nfrs.fitness_function: Real-branch integration test: seed stock rows for a SKU at multiple locations, request the detail view, assert every seeded (location, quantity, tracking code) appears; layering fitness test asserts the read path goes boundary -> service -> repository.
